questions.question_text: What must all Tennessee real estate advertisements include?
questions.correct_answer: The brokerage firm name as registered with TREC
distractors: The affiliate broker's personal phone number
The TREC license number only
The principal broker's home address
questions.explanation: All advertising must include the name of the brokerage firm as registered with TREC. This applies to all forms of advertising — print, online, signs, and social media.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker places an ad for a listing that shows only the affiliate's name and phone number. What violation has occurred?
questions.correct_answer: This is a prohibited blind ad
distractors: No violation if the listing is accurate
Acceptable for online ads only
Only a violation for commercial property
questions.explanation: A 'blind ad' omits the firm name, which is prohibited in Tennessee. All ads must identify the brokerage firm, even when the affiliate broker's name also appears.

questions.question_text: A brokerage changes its registered name with TREC. What must happen to existing advertisements?
questions.correct_answer: They must be updated to show the new name
distractors: Old ads are grandfathered for one year
Only new ads need the current name
TREC will update the ads automatically
questions.explanation: All advertising must include the firm name as currently registered with TREC. After a name change, existing advertisements must be updated to reflect the registered name.

questions.question_text: Which of the following would constitute a blind ad in Tennessee?
questions.correct_answer: A flyer listing only the agent's name and phone
distractors: A yard sign with the firm name and agent name
A website page showing the firm logo prominently
An MLS listing with firm details included
questions.explanation: An ad that identifies only the individual agent without the firm name is a blind ad, prohibited by TREC rules. The firm name must appear in all advertising.

questions.question_text: A licensee wants to advertise another broker's listing. Under what condition is this allowed?
questions.correct_answer: Only with the listing broker's permission
distractors: It is always prohibited in Tennessee
Only if the MLS allows co-advertising
Only if the property is over $500,000
questions.explanation: Advertising another broker's listing without permission is prohibited. However, with the listing broker's consent, a licensee may advertise the property as long as the firm name requirement is met.

questions.question_text: What information must a 'For Sale' sign in Tennessee include?
questions.correct_answer: The brokerage firm name
distractors: The affiliate broker's license number
The property's assessed tax value
The TREC complaint hotline number
questions.explanation: For Sale signs must include the brokerage firm name. The affiliate broker's name may also appear, but the firm name is the mandatory element.

questions.question_text: A real estate team places a yard sign showing only their team name, 'Nashville Dream Team.' Is this compliant?
questions.correct_answer: No, the brokerage firm name must also appear
distractors: Yes, team names are sufficient for signs
Yes, if the team is registered with TREC
Only if the team has its own license
questions.explanation: Team advertising must include the brokerage firm name. A team name alone, without the firm name, is not compliant with TREC advertising rules.

questions.question_text: On a For Sale sign, an affiliate broker's name appears in larger font than the firm name. Is this a violation?
questions.correct_answer: The firm name must be prominent on all signs
distractors: No violation as long as both names appear
Only a violation on commercial properties
Acceptable if the broker approves the design
questions.explanation: While both names may appear, the firm name must be prominently displayed. If the affiliate's name overshadows the firm name, it may not meet TREC's advertising requirements.

questions.question_text: A team within a brokerage wants to use their own logo on marketing materials. What additional element is required?
questions.correct_answer: The brokerage firm name must be included
distractors: TREC approval of the team logo
A separate team license from TREC
The principal broker's personal signature
questions.explanation: Team marketing materials must always include the brokerage firm name, regardless of whether the team uses its own logo or branding.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker places a directional sign to an open house. Must it include the firm name?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, all advertising signs require the firm name
distractors: No, directional signs are exempt
Only if the sign includes pricing info
Only if the sign is on public property
questions.explanation: All real estate advertising, including directional and open house signs, must include the brokerage firm name. There is no exemption for directional signs under TREC rules.

questions.question_text: Do Tennessee advertising rules apply to social media posts about listings?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, the same rules apply as traditional ads
distractors: No, social media is exempt from ad rules
Only if the post is a paid promotion
Only if the post includes pricing
questions.explanation: Social media advertising is subject to the same TREC rules as traditional advertising. This includes the requirement to display the firm name in all posts about listings.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker creates a personal real estate website. What must the website include?
questions.correct_answer: The brokerage firm name
distractors: Only the affiliate's license number
The TREC website link
The affiliate's education history
questions.explanation: Any internet advertising, including personal websites, must include the brokerage firm name. This applies to all online platforms where the licensee markets real estate services.

questions.question_text: A licensee posts a video tour of a listing on social media without mentioning the firm name anywhere. What should they do?
questions.correct_answer: Add the firm name to the post or video
distractors: Nothing, video content is exempt
Request a social media waiver from TREC
Only add it if someone asks in comments
questions.explanation: All advertising, including video content on social media, must identify the brokerage firm. The licensee must add the firm name to comply with TREC advertising rules.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker posts another firm's listing on their personal Instagram without permission. What violation occurred?
questions.correct_answer: Advertising another broker's listing without consent
distractors: No violation for social media sharing
Only a violation if they claim it as their own
Permitted if they tag the listing agent
questions.explanation: Advertising another broker's listing without permission is prohibited regardless of the medium. Social media sharing of listings requires the listing broker's consent.

questions.question_text: A licensee's online listing advertisement must contain which required information?
questions.correct_answer: Information as specified by TREC rules
distractors: Only the listing price and address
The property's complete tax history
The seller's reason for selling
questions.explanation: Advertised listings must contain required information as specified by TREC rules, which includes the firm name and accurate property information. Listing ads must be truthful and not misleading.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker offers a $100 gift card to anyone who signs a listing agreement. Is this allowed?
questions.correct_answer: No, gifts cannot be contingent on a contract
distractors: Yes, if the gift is under $250
Yes, if disclosed in the listing agreement
Yes, if the principal broker approves
questions.explanation: Under TREC rules, gifts and prizes cannot be contingent on entering into a contract. This protects consumers from being induced into real estate transactions by non-real-estate incentives.

questions.question_text: A brokerage hosts an open house and offers free refreshments. Does this violate TREC gift rules?
questions.correct_answer: No, refreshments at an open house are acceptable
distractors: Yes, any gift at a showing is prohibited
Only if the refreshments cost over $50
Only if attendees must sign a contract
questions.explanation: General hospitality like refreshments at an open house is acceptable. The prohibition is on gifts or prizes contingent on entering into a real estate contract.

questions.question_text: A firm offers a drawing for a vacation trip at a home show. Winners must sign a listing agreement to claim the prize. Is this compliant?
questions.correct_answer: No, prizes cannot require entering a contract
distractors: Yes, drawings are exempt from TREC rules
Yes, if the prize value is under $1,000
Yes, if the terms are in the drawing rules
questions.explanation: Prize promotions that require entering into a real estate contract as a condition of claiming the prize violate TREC rules. Prizes must not be tied to contractual obligations.

questions.question_text: What general standard applies to all real estate advertising in Tennessee?
questions.correct_answer: All advertising must be truthful and not misleading
distractors: Advertising must be pre-approved by TREC
Only print advertising is regulated
Advertising rules apply to brokers only
questions.explanation: Tennessee requires all advertising to be truthful and not misleading. This overarching standard applies to every form of advertising by any licensee, not just specific types.

questions.question_text: A brokerage advertises 'guaranteed sale or we buy your home.' Under what condition might this violate TREC rules?
questions.correct_answer: If the guarantee terms are misleading
distractors: Guaranteed sale programs are always prohibited
Only if the guarantee applies to new homes
Only if the brokerage is a franchise
questions.explanation: The key standard is truthfulness. If the guaranteed sale advertisement contains misleading terms or conditions, it violates TREC's requirement that all advertising be truthful and not misleading.
